new_classes.definition_en: A liquid material entity intended for direct human consumption as a drink, covered by Chapter 22 of the EU Combined Nomenclature, excluding products rendered unsuitable for consumption as beverages by preparation for culinary purposes, sea water, distilled or conductivity water, acetic acid, and medicaments.
Wine which, when kept at a temperature of 20 °C in closed containers, has an excess pressure of not less than 3 bar; classified under CN subheading 2204 10 per Chapter 22 subheading note 1.

Water, including mineral waters and aerated waters, containing added sugar or other sweetening matter or flavoured, presented for direct consumption as a beverage; covered by CN subheading 2202 10 00 per Chapter 22 additional note 1.
A quality of a closed container of liquid representing the excess pressure (in bar) at 20 °C, used to discriminate sparkling wine (≥ 3 bar) from still wine under CN subheading note 1 for 2204 10.

new_properties.label_en: is for direct consumption as beverage

coverage_explanation:
Covered criteria:
1. Sparkling wine definition (subheading note 1): modelled via ExcessPressureQuality with a minInclusive 3.0 bar restriction on SparklingWine — fully captured.
2. Sweetened/flavoured water for direct consumption (additional note 1 / 2202 10 00): modelled via SweetenedOrFlavouredWater with isForDirectConsumptionAsBeverage — fully captured.
3. Container volume threshold (general note: 2 L liquid volume, not capacity): the TBox already contains SmallContainer (< 2 L) and LargeContainer (≥ 2 L) classes that correctly refer to liquid volume; a representative parthood restriction is added to SparklingWine. No new class needed.
4. Chapter 22 scope exclusions (note 1a–d: culinary-prepared products, sea water, distilled water, acetic acid) and medicaments exclusion: these are negative/exclusion criteria that cannot be directly expressed as OWL class restrictions without introducing the excluded classes (e.g. SeaWater, DistilledWater, AceticAcid, Medicament). These exclusions are documented but not formally axiomatised — reducing coverage.
5. Additional note 2 (actual alcoholic strength by volume definitions for 2204, 2205, 2206 10): references existing eucn:alcoholByVolumePercent property; the detailed definitional text (potential alcohol, total alcohol, etc.) requires further subproperties not yet in scope, and the named headings/subheadings for which this applies are not yet modelled as individuals here — partially covered.
6. Section note on 'pellets': purely cross-cutting administrative definition not specific to Chapter 22 product discrimination — not axiomatised (coverage reduction reflected).
Gap summary: exclusion criteria, detailed alcohol-strength sub-definitions, and the pellets section note are not fully formalised.
